_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet-thinking)**_

The issue is clear: the GitHub MCP server (lines 52-65) requires Docker to run, but Docker Desktop is not running on your Windows machine.

## Problem

The GitHub MCP server uses a Docker container (`ghcr.io/github/github-mcp-server`) which requires Docker Desktop to be running. The error message shows:

```
open //./pipe/dockerDesktopLinuxEngine: The system cannot find the file specified
```

## Solutions

**Option 1: Start Docker Desktop (simplest)**
- Launch Docker Desktop on Windows
- Wait for it to fully start
- Restart Cursor

**Option 2: Disable GitHub MCP (if not needed)**

_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet-thinking)**_

I've disabled the GitHub MCP server by adding `"disabled": true`. This will stop the Docker connection errors.

**What I did:**
- Added `"disabled": true` to the GitHub MCP server configuration
- This prevents Cursor from attempting to connect to the Docker container

**To re-enable later:**
1. Start Docker Desktop
2. Change `"disabled": true` to `"disabled": false` in `mcp.json`
3. Restart Cursor

The errors should stop appearing now. If you need GitHub MCP functionality, start Docker Desktop first.

[MCP Server Executables Explained: npx, uvx, Docker, and Beyond - DEV Community](https://dev.to/leomarsh/mcp-server-executables-explained-npx-uvx-docker-and-beyond-1i1n)\n2. [Connection failures with npm-based MCP servers while uvx-based servers work correctly (Solved, see the comment in this post) · Issue #76 · modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/issues/76)\n3. [geelen/mcp-remote](https://github.com/geelen/mcp-remote)\n4. [@teolin/mcp-github v3.3.6](https://registry.npmjs.org/%40teolin%2Fmcp-github)\n5. [Running MCP Servers Directly from GitHub - Rok Garbas](https://garbas.si/posts/mcp-servers-from-github/)\n\n# GitHub MCP Server Alternatives to Docker (2026)\n\nBased on current MCP server execution methods, here are the main alternatives to Docker:\n\n## Direct Execution Methods\n\n**npx (Node.js servers)** - Downloads and runs JavaScript/TypeScript MCP servers from npm without installation.[1] Simple to configure but requires Node.js installed and downloads on each fresh run.[1]\n\n**uvx (Python servers)** - The Python equivalent that manages dependencies in virtual environments automatically.[1] Part of the uv package manager and faster than pip for dependency resolution.[1]\n\n**Native Binaries** - Some MCP servers compile to native executables in Go, Rust, or C++, allowing direct execution with absolute paths.[1]\n\n**Global npm Installation** - Installing packages globally and using absolute paths to Node executables and server scripts provides a more reliable alternative to npx, particularly on Windows with NVM.[2]\n\n## Remote Alternative\n\n**mcp-remote** - A tool that bridges local stdio-only MCP clients to remote servers without Docker, supporting OAuth authentication and custom headers.[3] This allows running servers remotely while maintaining compatibility with clients that only support local execution.[3]\n\n## Key Advantages vs Docker\n\nUnlike Docker, these methods avoid container startup overhead and don't require Docker installation, though they lack Docker's resource limitation and sandboxing capabilities.[1]",

[github-mcp v0.0.7](https://registry.npmjs.org/github-mcp)\n\n# GitHub MCP Server npm Package Installation\n\nThe primary npm package for the official GitHub MCP Server is **@modelcontextprotocol/server-github**, with the latest version being v2025.4.8.[1] However, note that **development has moved to GitHub** at http://github.com/github/github-mcp-server.[1]\n\n## Installation Methods\n\n**Via npm:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnpm install @modelcontextprotocol/server-github\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAlternatively, there's a community package **github-mcp v0.0.7** available, which can be installed with:[5]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnpm install github-mcp\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOr globally:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnpm install -g github-mcp\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## About the Package\n\nThe official package provides an MCP server for the GitHub API with features including:[1]\n- File operations and repository management\n- Automatic branch creation\n- Search functionality for repositories, code, issues, and users\n- Issue and pull request management\n- Repository forking and creation\n\nThe official GitHub MCP Server is maintained by GitHub and is the recommended version for most users.[2][3]\n\n**Note:** The search results don't contain information specific to 2026 releases, as the current latest version is from 2025.",

Updated the GitHub MCP server configuration to use npx with the official npm package `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`.

**Changes:**
- Replaced `docker` command with `npx`
- Changed args to use `-y` flag (auto-confirm) with `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`
- Kept your GitHub token in env variables
- Removed the `disabled` flag

**Benefits:**
- No Docker Desktop required
- Faster startup (no container overhead)
- Automatic dependency management via npx
- Uses official GitHub MCP package (v2025.4.8)

Restart Cursor and the GitHub MCP server should work without Docker errors.
